title: Assessing the threat of marine debris

phrases.def: 使联想起；使浮现于脑海

sentences.en: Chelsea Rochman, an ecologist at the University of California, Davis, has been trying to answer a dismal question: Is everything terrible, or are things just very, very bad?
sentences.zh: 加州大学戴维斯分校的生态学家切尔西·罗奇曼一直在试图回答一个令人沮丧的问题：一切是否都糟透了，还是说情况只是非常、非常糟糕？

grammar.type: 同位语 + 冒号引出并列疑问
grammar.note: an ecologist at the University of California, Davis 为 Chelsea Rochman 的同位语；冒号后为两个并列的选择疑问句，作 question 的具体内容。

sentences.en: Rochman is a member of the National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis’s marine-debris working group, a collection of scientists who study, among other things, the growing problem of marine debris, also known as ocean trash.
sentences.zh: 罗奇曼是国家生态分析与综合中心海洋垃圾工作组的成员，该工作组是一群科学家的集合，他们研究的课题之一便是日益严重的海洋垃圾（又称海洋废弃物）问题。

grammar.type: 同位语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: a collection of scientists 为 working group 的同位语；who study... 为定语从句；also known as ocean trash 为过去分词短语作后置定语；among other things 为插入语，意为 “除其他事物外”。

sentences.en: Plenty of studies have sounded alarm bells about the state of marine debris; in a recent paper published in the journal Ecology, Rochman and her colleagues set out to determine how many of those perceived risks are real.
sentences.zh: 大量研究已就海洋垃圾的现状敲响警钟；在近期发表于《生态学》（Ecology）期刊的一篇论文中，罗奇曼及其同事着手确定这些被认为存在的风险中有多少是真实的。

grammar.type: 分号并列 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: 分号连接两个独立分句；published in the journal Ecology 为过去分词短语作定语；set out to do 意为 “着手做”；how many of those perceived risks are real 为宾语从句。

sentences.en: Often, Rochman says, scientists will end a paper by speculating about the broader impacts of what they’ve found.
sentences.zh: 罗奇曼说，科学家们常常会在论文结尾推测他们的发现所带来的更广泛影响。

grammar.type: 插入语 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: Rochman says 为插入语；by speculating about... 为方式状语；what they've found 为 about 的宾语从句。

words.def: 更广泛的（broad 的比较级）

sentences.en: For example, a study could show that certain seabirds eat plastic bags, and go on to warn that whole bird populations are at risk of dying out.
sentences.zh: 例如，某项研究可能会表明某些海鸟会吞食塑料袋，进而警告说整个鸟类种群都面临灭绝的风险。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: 主语 a study 带两个并列谓语 could show... 和 go on to warn...；each 后各接一个 that 宾语从句；go on to do 意为 “接着做”；at risk of 意为 “有……的危险”。

sentences.en: ‘But the truth was that nobody had yet tested those perceived threats,’ Rochman says. ‘There wasn’t a lot of information.’
sentences.zh: “但事实是，还没有人检验过这些被认为存在的威胁，”罗奇曼说，“相关信息并不多。”

grammar.type: 直接引语 + 表语从句 + 过去完成时
grammar.note: the truth was that... 为表语从句；had yet tested 用过去完成时表 “（当时）尚未检验”；yet 强调 “还没有”。

sentences.en: Rochman and her colleagues examined more than a hundred papers on the impacts of marine debris that were published through 2013.
sentences.zh: 罗奇曼及其同事查阅了截至2013年发表的一百多篇关于海洋垃圾影响的论文。

grammar.type: 定语从句
grammar.note: that were published through 2013 为定语从句修饰 papers；through 2013 意为 “直到2013年（含）”。

sentences.en: Within each paper, they asked what threats scientists had studied – 366 perceived threats in all – and what they’d actually found.
sentences.zh: 在每篇论文中，他们都会考察科学家研究了哪些威胁——总共366项被认为存在的威胁——以及他们实际发现了什么。

grammar.type: 并列宾语从句 + 破折号插入
grammar.note: asked 后接两个并列宾语从句 what threats... 和 what they'd actually found；两个破折号之间为插入成分补充说明数量；in all 意为 “总共”。

sentences.en: In 83 percent of cases, the perceived dangers of ocean trash were proven true.
sentences.zh: 在83%的案例中，人们认为海洋垃圾存在的危险被证实是真实的。

grammar.type: 被动语态
grammar.note: were proven true 为被动结构，prove 此处作 “证明（是）” 讲，true 为宾补充当主补。

sentences.en: In the remaining cases, the working group found the studies had weaknesses in design and content which affected the validity of their conclusions – they lacked a control group, for example, or used faulty statistics.
sentences.zh: 在其余的案例中，工作组发现这些研究在设计和内容上存在缺陷，影响了其结论的有效性——例如缺少对照组，或使用了有误的统计方法。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + 定语从句 + 破折号举例
grammar.note: found (that) the studies had... 为宾语从句；which affected... 为定语从句修饰 weaknesses；破折号后为举例说明，含两个并列谓语 lacked... 和 used...。

sentences.en: Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits.
sentences.zh: 罗奇曼说，引人注目的是，只有一项设计良好的研究未能发现它所寻找的效应，那是一项关于贻贝吞食微小塑料颗粒的调查。

grammar.type: 同位语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: an investigation of mussels... 为前面 one well-designed study 的同位语；(that) it was looking for 为省略关系词的定语从句修饰 effect；ingesting... 为现在分词作定语修饰 mussels；fail to do 意为 “未能做”。

words.def: 微小的；显微镜才可见的

sentences.en: The plastic moved from the mussels’ stomachs to their bloodstreams, scientists found, and stayed there for weeks – but didn’t seem to stress out the shellfish.
sentences.zh: 科学家发现，塑料从贻贝的胃部进入其血液，并在那里停留了数周——但似乎并未使这些贝类感到压力。

grammar.type: 插入语 + 并列谓语 + 转折
grammar.note: scientists found 为插入语；主语 The plastic 带并列谓语 moved... 和 stayed...；破折号后 but didn't seem to... 为转折；stress out 意为 “使……焦虑/紧张”。

sentences.en: While mussels may be fine eating trash, though, the analysis also gave a clearer picture of the many ways that ocean debris is bothersome.
sentences.zh: 不过，尽管贻贝吞食垃圾或许无碍，但这项分析也更清晰地揭示了海洋垃圾在诸多方面确实造成的麻烦。

grammar.type: 让步状语从句 + 定语从句
grammar.note: While 引导让步状语从句；though 为插入的转折副词；that ocean debris is bothersome 为定语从句修饰 ways（关系副词 that = in which）；give a clearer picture of 意为 “更清楚地呈现”。

sentences.en: Within the studies they looked at, most of the proven threats came from plastic debris, rather than other materials like metal or wood.
sentences.zh: 在他们所研究的那些论文中，大多数已被证实的威胁来自塑料垃圾，而非金属或木材等其他材料。

grammar.type: 省略定语从句 + rather than 并列
grammar.note: (that) they looked at 为省略关系词的定语从句修饰 studies；rather than other materials 表 “而不是”；like metal or wood 举例。

sentences.en: Most of the dangers also involved large pieces of debris – animals getting entangled in trash, for example, or eating it and severely injuring themselves.
sentences.zh: 大多数危险还涉及大块的垃圾——例如动物被垃圾缠住，或吞食垃圾而使自己严重受伤。

grammar.type: 破折号举例 + 独立主格
grammar.note: 破折号后 animals getting entangled... 与 (animals)... eating it and... 为并列的 “名词 + 现在分词” 独立主格结构作举例说明；get entangled in 意为 “被……缠住”。

sentences.en: But a lot of ocean debris is ‘microplastic’, or pieces smaller than five millimeters.
sentences.zh: 但很多海洋垃圾是“微塑料”，即小于五毫米的碎片。

grammar.type: 同位语（or 解释）
grammar.note: or pieces smaller than five millimeters 由 or 引出，对 microplastic 作解释说明（同位）；smaller than 为比较级作后置定语。

sentences.en: These may be ingredients used in cosmetics and toiletries, fibers shed by synthetic clothing in the wash, or eroded remnants of larger debris.
sentences.zh: 这些微塑料可能是化妆品和洗漱用品中使用的成分、合成衣物在洗涤时脱落的纤维，或是较大垃圾被侵蚀后的残余物。

grammar.type: 并列表语 + 过去分词定语
grammar.note: may be 后接三个并列表语；used in cosmetics... 、shed by synthetic clothing... 、eroded... 均为过去分词短语作定语修饰各自的中心词。

sentences.en: Compared to the number of studies investigating large-scale debris, Rochman’s group found little research on the effects of these tiny bits.
sentences.zh: 与调查大尺度垃圾的研究数量相比，罗奇曼的团队发现关于这些微小碎片影响的研究少之又少。

grammar.type: 过去分词状语 + 现在分词定语
grammar.note: Compared to... 为过去分词短语作状语表比较；investigating large-scale debris 为现在分词短语作定语修饰 studies；little research 表 “很少的研究”。

sentences.en: ‘There are a lot of open questions still for microplastic,’ Rochman says, though she notes that more papers on the subject have been published since 2013, the cutoff point for the group’s analysis.
sentences.zh: “关于微塑料仍有许多悬而未决的问题，”罗奇曼说，不过她指出，自2013年——即该团队分析的截止点——以来，有关这一主题的论文已发表了更多。

grammar.type: 直接引语 + 让步从句 + 宾语从句 + 同位语
grammar.note: though she notes that... 为让步状语从句，其中 that... 为宾语从句；the cutoff point for the group's analysis 为 2013 的同位语；open questions 意为 “尚未解决的问题”。

words.def: 尚无定论的问题；悬而未决的问题

sentences.en: There are also, she adds, a lot of open questions about the ways that ocean debris can lead to sea-creature death.
sentences.zh: 她补充说，关于海洋垃圾如何导致海洋生物死亡，也存在许多尚未解答的问题。

grammar.type: 插入语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: she adds 为插入语；that ocean debris can lead to sea-creature death 为定语从句修饰 ways；lead to 意为 “导致”。

sentences.en: Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal’s tissues or cells, rather than whole populations.
sentences.zh: 许多研究考察的是塑料如何影响单个动物，或该动物的组织或细胞，而非整个种群。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + rather than 并列
grammar.note: how plastic affects... 为宾语从句；从句内 an individual animal 与 that animal's tissues or cells 为并列宾语；rather than whole populations 表对比。

sentences.en: And in the lab, scientists often use higher concentrations of plastic than what’s really in the ocean.
sentences.zh: 而且在实验室里，科学家使用的塑料浓度往往高于海洋中实际存在的浓度。

grammar.type: 比较结构
grammar.note: higher... than what's really in the ocean 为比较结构，than 后接 what 引导的宾语从句作比较对象。

sentences.en: None of that tells us how many birds or fish or sea turtles could die from plastic pollution – or how deaths in one species could affect that animal’s predators, or the rest of the ecosystem.
sentences.zh: 这些都无法告诉我们有多少鸟类、鱼类或海龟可能死于塑料污染——也无法告诉我们某一物种的死亡会如何影响该动物的捕食者，或生态系统的其余部分。

grammar.type: 并列宾语从句
grammar.note: tells us 后接两个由破折号连接的并列宾语从句 how many birds... could die... 和 how deaths in one species could affect...；None of that 作主语表全否定。

sentences.en: ‘We need to be asking more ecologically relevant questions,’ Rochman says.
sentences.zh: “我们需要提出更多在生态学上有意义的问题，”罗奇曼说。

grammar.type: 直接引语
grammar.note: need to be asking 用不定式进行式强调 “正需要持续地问”；ecologically relevant 意为 “与生态相关的”。

sentences.en: Usually, scientists don’t know exactly how disasters such as a tanker accidentally spilling its whole cargo of oil and polluting huge areas of the ocean will affect the environment until after they’ve happened.
sentences.zh: 通常，直到灾难发生之后，科学家才确切知道诸如油轮意外泄漏其全部原油、污染大片海域这类事件将如何影响环境。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + not...until
grammar.note: how disasters... will affect the environment 为宾语从句；such as a tanker... spilling... and polluting... 以并列动名词短语举例说明 disasters；not... until after they've happened 表 “直到……之后才”。

sentences.en: ‘We don’t ask the right questions early enough,’ she says.
sentences.zh: “我们没有足够早地提出正确的问题，”她说。

grammar.type: 直接引语
grammar.note: early enough 中 enough 修饰副词 early 后置；the right questions 意为 “恰当的问题”。

sentences.en: But if ecologists can understand how the slow-moving effect of ocean trash is damaging ecosystems, they might be able to prevent things from getting worse.
sentences.zh: 但如果生态学家能够理解海洋垃圾这种缓慢发生的影响是如何损害生态系统的，他们或许就能防止情况进一步恶化。

grammar.type: 条件状语从句 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: if 引导条件状语从句，其中 how the slow-moving effect... is damaging... 为宾语从句；prevent sth from doing 意为 “阻止某事发生”。

words.def: 缓慢发生的；移动缓慢的

sentences.en: Asking the right questions can help policy makers, and the public, figure out where to focus their attention.
sentences.zh: 提出正确的问题有助于政策制定者和公众弄清应把注意力集中在何处。

grammar.type: 动名词主语 + help sb do
grammar.note: 动名词短语 Asking the right questions 作主语；help sb do 结构，figure out 为省略 to 的不定式；where to focus their attention 为 “疑问词 + 不定式” 作宾语。

sentences.en: The problems that look or sound most dramatic may not be the best places to start.
sentences.zh: 那些看起来或听起来最耸人听闻的问题，未必是最佳的着手之处。

grammar.type: 定语从句
grammar.note: that look or sound most dramatic 为定语从句修饰 problems；may not 表 “未必、不一定”。

words.def: 戏剧性的；引人注目的；耸人听闻的

sentences.en: For example, the name of the ‘Great Pacific Garbage Patch’ – a collection of marine debris in the northern Pacific Ocean – might conjure up a vast, floating trash island.
sentences.zh: 例如，“太平洋大垃圾带”这个名称——它是北太平洋中一片海洋垃圾的聚集区——可能会让人联想到一座巨大的、漂浮着的垃圾岛。

grammar.type: 破折号同位语
grammar.note: 两个破折号之间 a collection of marine debris... 为 Great Pacific Garbage Patch 的同位语；conjure up 意为 “使浮现于脑海、使联想到”。

words.def: 使联想起；使浮现于脑海

sentences.en: In reality though, much of the debris is tiny or below the surface; a person could sail through the area without seeing any trash at all.
sentences.zh: 然而实际上，大部分垃圾都很微小或位于水面之下；一个人可能航行穿过该区域却根本看不到任何垃圾。

grammar.type: 分号并列 + 介词短语状语
grammar.note: In reality though 为插入的转折；分号连接两个独立分句；without seeing any trash at all 为伴随/方式状语，at all 强化否定。

sentences.en: A Dutch group called ‘The Ocean Cleanup’ is currently working on plans to put mechanical devices in the Pacific Garbage Patch and similar areas to suck up plastic.
sentences.zh: 一个名为“海洋清理”（The Ocean Cleanup）的荷兰团体目前正在制定计划，打算在太平洋垃圾带及类似区域安放机械装置以吸走塑料。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 不定式定语/目的状语
grammar.note: called 'The Ocean Cleanup' 为过去分词短语作定语修饰 group；to put mechanical devices... 为不定式作 plans 的定语；to suck up plastic 为目的状语。

sentences.en: But a recent paper used simulations to show that strategically positioning the cleanup devices closer to shore would more effectively reduce pollution over the long term.
sentences.zh: 但近期一篇论文用模拟实验表明，从长远看，把清理装置有策略地布置在更靠近海岸的位置，能更有效地减少污染。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + 动名词主语
grammar.note: to show that... 为目的状语，that 引导宾语从句；从句主语为动名词短语 strategically positioning the cleanup devices closer to shore；over the long term 意为 “从长远看”。

sentences.en: ‘I think clearing up some of these misperceptions is really important,’ Rochman says.
sentences.zh: “我认为澄清其中一些误解真的很重要，”罗奇曼说。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + 动名词主语
grammar.note: I think 后接省略 that 的宾语从句；从句主语为动名词短语 clearing up some of these misperceptions；clear up 意为 “澄清、消除”。

sentences.en: Among scientists as well as in the media, she says, ‘A lot of the images about strandings and entanglement and all of that cause the perception that plastic debris is killing everything in the ocean.’
sentences.zh: 她说，无论是在科学界还是在媒体上，“大量关于（海洋生物）搁浅、缠绕之类的图像，都造成了一种印象，即塑料垃圾正在杀死海洋中的一切”。

grammar.type: 插入状语 + 同位语从句
grammar.note: Among scientists as well as in the media 为地点状语前置，she says 为插入语；the perception that plastic debris is killing... 中 that 引导同位语从句解释 perception。

sentences.en: Interrogating the existing scientific literature can help ecologists figure out which problems really need addressing, and which ones they’d be better off – like the mussels – absorbing and ignoring.
sentences.zh: 细查现有的科学文献能帮助生态学家弄清哪些问题确实需要解决，而哪些问题——就像贻贝那样——他们最好去承受并忽略。

grammar.type: 动名词主语 + 并列宾语从句
grammar.note: 动名词短语 Interrogating the existing scientific literature 作主语；help ecologists figure out 后接两个并列宾语从句 which problems... 和 which ones...；would be better off doing 意为 “做……会更好”；like the mussels 为破折号插入的类比。

questions.title: Questions 27–33
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 27–33 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

items.prompt: Rochman and her colleagues were the first people to research the problem of marine debris.
The creatures most in danger from ocean trash are certain seabirds.
The studies Rochman has reviewed have already proved that populations of some birds will soon become extinct.
Rochman analysed papers on the different kinds of danger caused by ocean trash.
Most of the research analysed by Rochman and her colleagues was badly designed.
One study examined by Rochman was expecting to find that mussels were harmed by eating plastic.
Some mussels choose to eat plastic in preference to their natural diet.

questions.title: Questions 34–39
instructions: Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 34–39 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: Studies of marine debris found the biggest threats were: plastic (not metal or wood); bits of debris that were __________ (harmful to animals).
There was little research into __________ e.g. from synthetic fibres.
Drawbacks of the studies examined: most of them focused on individual animals, not entire __________.
the __________ of plastic used in the lab did not always reflect those in the ocean.
the impact of a reduction in numbers on the __________ of that species.
Rochman says more information is needed on the possible impact of future __________ (e.g. involving oil).

questions.title: Question 40
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in box 40 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt:
What would be the best title for this passage?
A  Assessing the threat of marine debris
B  Marine debris: who is to blame?
C  A new solution to the problem of marine debris
D  Marine debris: the need for international action
